brief_notes: Clear and well-structured study demonstrating that caspase-11 catalytic activity and autoprocessing are required for noncanonical inflammasome SMOC assembly, challenging the prevailing passive-recruitment model. First direct visualization of caspase-11 specks in macrophages. Strengths: unexpected mechanistic finding with strong conceptual impact, multiple orthogonal readouts (microscopy, SEC, GSDMD cleavage, LDH release, PI uptake), clear mutant panel design (WT, C254A, D285A), logical experimental progression, well-articulated revised model. Weaknesses: no figures, relies on overexpressed GFP-tagged constructs (acknowledged), no endogenous validation, speck quantification ranges (30-50%) suggest variability not fully characterized, no time-course kinetics beyond the 2-6h window, SEC lacks molecular weight standards reporting. The proposed positive feedback model is speculative and would benefit from additional mechanistic data.
